title: Gentle Chinese Lamb Century Egg Congee
description: A malty, silky, and romantic Chinese dish featuring lamb shoulder and century eggs, made with ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly ingredients, served in a ramekin.
instructions: In a large pot, heat the sesame oil over medium heat.
Add the grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the lamb shoulder and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the lamb broth, malt syrup, salt, and black pepper, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
While the lamb is cooking, prepare the congee by rinsing the Chinese rice and soaking it in water for 30 minutes.
Drain and rinse the rice, then add it to the pot with the lamb and broth.
Simmer, covered, for an additional 30 minutes, or until the congee is creamy and the liquid has been absorbed.
To serve, divide the congee among 4 ramekins, then top each with a slice of century egg, some chopped scallions, and a drizzle of malt syrup.
